target: 9. Island Stroll
Koyuba-san, sorry to keep you waiting.
I'm sorry, getting ready took a little longer than expected.
No, don't worry about it. It's my fault for showing up so suddenly.
Yeah, I was really surprised by how sudden it was.
I-I'm sorry. There was actually something I wanted to ask before we go...
Something you wanted to ask?
I originally thought I should wait until things settled down, but I have to go back to Tokyo right after the festival...
I'm really sorry for being so selfish, but...
I wanted to say hello to Alain-san, and also... to Ushio.
Ah, yeah, thanks. Alain's already at the cafe, so let's go see him.
Yeah...
Good morning, Alain.
Good morning. You're heading out somewhere with Mio?
Ah, yeah, but...
Um, good morning.
Ah, good morning. A friend of Shinpei or Mio's?
Ah, this is Koyuba-san. She went to the same elementary school as me and Ushio. She's back on the island for the first time in seven years.
I'm sorry for disturbing you while you're busy getting ready. My name is Kaori Koyuba.
Ah, the Koyuba family's! Well, well, thank you for the polite greeting.
I'm her father, Alain. I'm the owner of this Cafe Kofune.
Yes, I remember! When I lived on this island, my parents and I used to come eat here!
Haha, that's right. I remember that well too.
Really?! I'm so happy...
Mio, are the three of you heading out now?
Yeah, but before that, Kaori-chan wanted to see Sis...
Um... I wanted to offer some incense to Ushio-san. Is that alright?
Ah, of course. Ushio will be happy too.
Mio, can you show her the way?
Yeah.
Thank you. Excuse me.
That Koyuba girl... She's grown into quite the beauty.
Apparently she's working as an idol in Tokyo.
Oh, is that so?! That's amazing.
She asked me to be her guide to show her around the island's famous spots. I'll be heading out with Mio after this.
Ah, have fun. You don't need to worry about the cafe.
Thanks. Ah, but before we go, we might want to talk to you, Alain.
Talk? About what?
Koyuba-san is doing a presentation on Hitogashima at a talk show during the festival.
Because of that, we're going to tour the island's famous spots. She'll be asking the locals for stories wherever we go.
S-So, you're starting by hearing a story from me?!
Oh boy... What should I even talk about?!
She said she wants to hear raw opinions, so just answering casually should be fine.
(I'm sure Koyuba-san really wanted to offer incense to Ushio...)
(She's really a good kid... That's exactly why I have to at least prevent her from meeting a tragic fate.)
(After hearing Alain's stories, we visited a few famous spots based on Koyuba-san's requests...)
Wow, there's a spot where you can have such a beautiful view all to yourself? That's amazing!
Right? Keep it a secret from everyone else!
Thank you! I guess there really are quite a few hidden spots that only the locals know about.
Jotting this down...
That's exactly the kind of story I wanted to hear. I'm glad I reached out to you.
Hehehe... Feel free to talk to me anytime.
Yes! Thank you very much!
Somehow...
...Ah, excuse me! Do you mind if I ask you a few questions?
She's a bit different from the Kaori-chan I used to know...
Yeah, that's her \"idol mode.\"
Actually, I want to learn more about this island right now, so I've been going around asking the locals various things--
But... isn't she amazing? She's super energetic, or rather...
Y-Yeah... Did I really need to tag along?
Well, for now, it doesn't look like there's any room for us to step in...
Ah, Shinpei-kun!
Whoa!?
What was with that report yesterday!? It was a complete lie, wasn't it!?
A-About that...!
Tetsu-san, hello! Are you slacking off again?
Don't say such harsh things with a smile. I'm working properly right now. Can't you tell I'm on patrol?
Huh, Tetsu-san going on patrol in this heat is pretty rare. Did something happen?
Ah, about that. I just got a report from Shiomi-san--
Shinpei-kun, Mio-chan! Sorry to keep you waiting.
What, you weren't just here with Mio-chan?
Ah, hello. Are you the local police officer?
Ah, that's right. I'm this island's Hero of Justice... wait, huh?
This is Totsumura-san. The local police officer who calls himself a Hero of Justice.
Totsumura-san... yeah, I remember.
Nice to meet you, Totsumura-san. I'm Koyuba.
It's a pleasure to meet you!
Idol mode.
Mio, keep quiet...
Kaori-chan, looks like you were able to ask a lot of things.
Yeah! I made sure to take perfect notes.
Ah, that's good then.
More importantly, Totsumura-san, Kaori-chan greeted you but you're just standing there silently. Isn't that rude?
K-K-Koy... Koyukoyukoyu...
Shin-chan, what should we do? Totsumura-san broke.
Um, Totsumura-san?
K-Koyuba, you mean, could it be Kaori Koyuba-chan!?
Ah, yes. Did you happen to know me?
Know you? I'm a massive fan!!
I-I-It's the real deal!! Why is a super popular idol in a place like this...!?
Actually, I'm from Hitogashima... and I'm currently visiting my hometown.
I wanted to learn more about the island since it's been a while, so I was just having my classmate Shinpei-kun show me around.
F-From Hitogashima...? Shinpei-kun's classmate...?
A-Are you serious...! This is my chance!!
Yo... ho, hiyah...!
(W-Why is he suddenly fixing his appearance!?)
Nice to meet you, Kaori Koyuba-chan.
I am the only police officer on this island... or rather, the Hero of Justice, Tetsu Totsumura. A pleasure to make your acquaintance.
Hehe, nice to meet you, Hero of Justice.
Knowing that my hometown island is protected by a Hero of Justice like you, Totsumura-san, makes me feel very relieved.
Nwahahaha~n! What incredibly kind words!
Leave it to me! As long as this Totsumura, the Hero of Justice, is here, this island is safe and sound!
His movements are all squirmy.
He's probably trying to look cool...
I've been closely following all your activities, Kaori-chan!
TV of course, but also magazines, radio, and the internet...! You're getting massive attention everywhere!
Wow, I'm so happy!
Ah, that's right! An autograph! Please sign this notepad!
Isn't that your notepad for work?
Mio-chan, remember this well. There are mountains of things in this world that are far more important than work.
Even if you say that with such a smug look...
It was great talking to you, Totsumura-san. Thank you so much for letting me interview you...
No, thank YOU! You gave me a dream-like experience! I'll make this autograph a family heirloom!
Well then, I'll be taking my leave!
Yes, good luck with your work!
(Seeing Koyuba-san do fan service like that, it really hits me that she's an actual idol.)
(She's like a completely different person from the rather introverted Koyuba-san she used to be.)
U-Ugh... my stomach...
Where should we guide you to next?
Ah... Mio, hold on a sec. I'm thirsty, let's take a break around here.
Ah... right!
We've been outside this whole time, we should probably hydrate soon. Right, Kaori-chan?
Y-Yeah, I agree.
I'll go buy something from that vending machine. What do you two want?
Ah... since you're keeping me company, I'll buy them.
It's fine, it's fine.
Something refreshing for me.
That's pretty vague. What about you, Koyuba-san?
Ah, um... Then, iced tea for me.
Got it. Then wait for me in the shade over there.
Okaaay.
(Koyuba-san seems a bit tired...)
(I mean, I think I heard her say her stomach... earlier...)
(Could it be, she's actually not good at interacting with people like this?)
(No, she's an idol so she should be used to it...)
Here, sorry for the wait.
Thank you, Shinpei-kun.
Thanks for the drink~.
Koyuba-san, you were taking notes again?
Yeah, I figured I should write down what I heard from Totsumura-san before I forget.
You're so diligent, Kaori-chan. I'm honestly impressed.
No, it's nothing like that.
It definitely is. Shin-chan, you think so too, right?
I think so too.
How do you take your notes?
Yeah, I think so too.
W-Wow, even you, Shinpei-kun...
Even though you have a talk show, just having some general knowledge should be enough.
To organize it all so properly, I really think that's impressive.
It's nothing to be praised for. If I don't take notes, I just can't remember things...
And you're doing it properly without finding it a hassle, right? That's definitely impressive.
Y... you think so? Thank you.
How do you take your notes, Koyuba-san?
Eh... y-you want to see?
Yeah, if possible.
Ah, I want to see too!
Sure, but... don't expect too much, okay?
Here you go. See... I just <color=#GF_TIPS_073>note</color> down what I hear exactly as it is.
Wow, it's super neat!
Yeah, and it's really easy to understand.
(Come to think of it, I feel like the teacher praised her notes back in elementary school too...)
Based on these notes, I'm going to introduce all the great things about the island at the talk show...
I want everyone to come to love my favorite Hitogashima too.
That's really something to be grateful for.
Yeah!
Huh? This notepad...?
Isn't this character the same one as the keychain you showed me before?
Yeah, that's right. I also have a book cover and a card case... I end up buying them whenever I see them.
You've liked that since a long time ago, huh.
You remembered!? ...I'm glad.
Muu...
(Koyuba-san... she might seem different from before, but her core really hasn't changed.)
(Even though she's not that good at talking to people, she still worked hard to gather information about the island...)
(After resting for a while, we went around to various other places on the island and continued our interviews...)
(Finally, we arrived at Hitogashima Shrine.)
After all, when you think of Hitogashima, you think of Hiruko-sama.
Yeah, the summer festival is held here too.
(To be honest, I really wanted to avoid coming here on the 23rd...)
(But it's later than it was back then. Plus, this time, Mio doesn't know about the Kobayakawa family yet.)
(Even if she sees Shiori-chan's shadow, she probably won't chase after it immediately... I think.)
(Even if something happens, as long as we don't chase too far or go anywhere deserted, we should be fine.)
(Plus, Koyuba-san is with us this time... I need to stay on my guard.)
Oh, hello there. What an unexpected combination.
Hello, Karikiri-san.
Hello. Thank you so much for yesterday!
Eh, yesterday?
Karikiri-san also participated in the meeting for the live show.
Ah, is that so.
I only joined halfway through, though.
Well, since the live show is being held here at Hitogashima Shrine just like the festival, I was called in as the head priest.
I see, since you're the one in charge of this shrine.
(If that's the case... does that mean he rushed to that meeting right after the funeral?)
But now that I think about it, is it really okay to hold a live show at a shrine?
It's fine, it's fine. The gods aren't that narrow-minded.
Besides, for things like this, the ones who have fun win.
That's a very Karikiri-san way of thinking.
To begin with, I was actually the one who proposed inviting her for a live show to everyone at the Chamber of Commerce.
Eh?
You see, back in my college days, I was in Tokyo, right?
One time, I saw an idol belonging to a certain production agency performing outdoors while surrounded by a huge crowd of fans.
Do you know it? The one where that man called "God" is the general producer!
I couldn't forget how moved I was, so even after returning to the island, I kept checking up on the idols belonging to that agency.
That's when I learned of a wonderful idol named Kaori Koyuba-san. Seeing her immense popularity, and finding out she was from this island, I thought we absolutely had to invite her here--
K-Kaori-chan!! You had something you wanted to ask Karikiri-san, right!?
Oh, is that so? What might it be?
Y-Yes! Actually... I was hoping you could tell me some stories related to Hitogashima Shrine.
I thought it could serve as one of the topics for the talk show.
Of course, that's perfectly fine. Where should I start?
Ah, yes, well then--
(Nice one, Mio! We were dangerously close to being dragged into a really long story...)
Thank you very much. It was fun getting to hear so many different stories.
You listened with such deep interest, it was fun for me to talk about it too.
It seems I wasn't wrong to introduce you to the Chamber of Commerce after all.
I'm so happy to hear you say that!
Oh right, your stream last night was wonderful.
Kaori-chan, did you do some kind of stream last night?
Yeah, it's on my personal live streaming channel, but yesterday I did a stream from my room about the upcoming live show.
Come to think of it, you did mention that the live show on the day of the festival would be streamed online too.
Yeah, that's right.
I'm planning to announce the URL right before the secret live show, but I thought some people might end up missing it.
So, on my live streaming channel, I've been slowly dropping hints like "there's an event coming up soon."
Of course, the fact that it's on Hitogashima is kept a secret.
Oh, that's interesting. It'll probably build up the fans' anticipation too.
But at this point, wouldn't it be fine to just announce that it's being held on Hitogashima?
The festival is already tomorrow, so even if fans try to come to the island at this timing, I don't think there would be that many.
Haa... Shin-chan, you really don't get it at all, do you.
You can't underestimate idol fans! If it's for the idol they love, they'll use any means necessary to get to the island!
E-Eh?
You really are too naive, Shinpei-kun. If a massive crowd of people were to surge into Hitogashima Shrine, it could easily lead to a major accident.
I-Is it really that crazy?
Of course it is. Koyuba-san isn't just any idol. She's a famous, highly popular idol.
According to rumors, there were even fans who managed to pinpoint that it was Hitogashima using only the information from last night's live stream.
Oh, you didn't know?
Apparently, something like that was written in the comment section of your agency's official SNS account this morning.
I-Is that true...!? I'm so sorry, I never imagined they'd figure it out!
Please don't worry about it. Even if they do come, it'll likely only be a small fraction. I'm looking forward to the live show on the day.
Y... Yes.
Man... doing a live show seems a lot tougher than I thought.
If we had more time, I think there would've been ways to handle it, but...
Since this was such a sudden offer, neither the agency staff nor the islanders could prepare perfectly... you know?
Plus, the staff won't be able to make it to the island until tomorrow.
So that was the situation...
Yeah, this is a first for me.
But I was careless. I thought they'd never figure out it was Hitogashima.
In the stream, it only showed a bit of your room and outside the window, right?
If they could figure it out from just that, then that fan is just way too incredible.
You really shouldn't worry about it too much.
You think so...?
For now, just focus on the live show. Mio and I are both really looking forward to it.
Yeah... thank you. I feel a bit more energized now.
Muu...
Hey, Mio. Why are you walking so far away? Come over here.
It's fine. I'm just making sure I don't get in your way.
Huh? I couldn't hear what you said.
I said it's fine! It's nothing!
...Jeez.
It's not nothing. We don't know when a Shadow might show up, so don't wander away from me.
I-I get it, so... could you step back a little bit?
A-Ah.
...Hehe.
Watching you two interact just now made me feel a bit nostalgic.
What do you mean?
Whenever little Mio-chan would wander away from the group, Shinpei-kun would run over to her and pull her by the hand, telling her to come back.
I was just thinking how it hasn't really changed.
Y-You remember something from that long ago so well...
Yeah, I remember it well. Let's see, what else...
Oh right, back in elementary school, there was a time I happened to walk home together with Shinpei-kun and Kofune-san.
At that time, Mio-chan was walking in front and suddenly tripped and fell.
Eh, I did?
And seeing that, Shinpei-kun and Kofune-san started competing to see who could help Mio-chan up faster.
Once that was settled, the two of them started clashing again over who would get to give Mio-chan a piggyback ride home.
And then... what happened?
In the end, Mio-chan didn't want a piggyback ride, so we walked home holding hands with Mio-chan in the middle.
D-Did that really happen?
T-That does sound like something that could've happened... But I don't remember it at all.
Hehe... it's kind of strange. To think I'm the only one who remembers that.
Back when I was on Hitogashima... Everything reflected in my eyes looked so sparkling and bright.
You know, when I came back to this island, I felt like that sparkle returned to me.
And then I thought to myself. This island really is wonderful...
And I really, really love this island.
That's why, being able to spend this casual time touring the island like this makes me incredibly happy.
Yeah... those feelings of yours have definitely reached us too.
You really do love Hitogashima, Kaori-chan.
Yeah, and that's exactly why I want to make tomorrow's event a definite success.
I see...
...I'll help too.
Eh?
I'll help out with the live show too!
I don't know what I can do, but I'll do anything, so please don't hesitate to put me to work!
I-I'm happy to hear that, but... why so suddenly?
After hearing what you just said... I just couldn't stay quiet.
Mio-chan...
It's not just Mio. I feel the same way.
If there's anything we can do to help, just let us know.
Yeah, exactly! We'll lend you a hand!
You two... Thank you!!
Thanks for keeping me company today.
It was super fun!
Be careful on your way back.
Byeee! See you tomorrow!
Yeah! See you tomorrow!
Well, let's head home early today to prepare for tomorrow's live show.
Yeah. Still, this was my first time seeing a live rehearsal.
It's not something ordinary people get to see very often, so it'll probably make for a good memory.
A memory, huh...
Did something happen?
No... Just thinking about that story of you falling earlier.
Koyuba-san really remembers things well, even the stuff I've completely forgotten.
As I thought, Kaori-chan really does... have feelings for Shin-chan...
I see... Since that long ago...
What is it? If you have something to say, just say it clearly.
I said it's nothing! You're seriously so dense...
